description: Create the complete Tic Tac Toe game as a Cargo project at /projects/sandbox/Kiro-Test. The game is a terminal/CLI two-player game using only the Rust standard library.
steps: Step 1: Initialize the Cargo project at /projects/sandbox/Kiro-Test by creating Cargo.toml and src/main.rs. The Cargo.toml should have name = 'tic-tac-toe', version = '0.1.0', edition = '2021', and NO dependencies (empty [dependencies] section). Do NOT run 'cargo init' as it may fail with existing files - create the files manually.

Step 2: Implement the game board data structure. Use an enum for cell state (Empty, X, O) and a Board struct holding a 3x3 grid (e.g., [[Cell; 3]; 3]). Implement Display trait for the Board to show a visual representation in the terminal with row/column labels for easy input. The board display should be clean and readable, e.g.:
  1 | 2 | 3
 -----------
  4 | 5 | 6
 -----------
  7 | 8 | 9
with X and O replacing numbers when played.

Step 3: Implement game logic functions: (a) check_winner() - checks all 8 win conditions (3 rows, 3 columns, 2 diagonals) and returns the winning player or None. (b) is_draw() - returns true if board is full with no winner. (c) make_move(position, player) - places a mark if the cell is empty, returns Result<(), error> for invalid moves. Players input a number 1-9 corresponding to board positions.

Step 4: Implement the main game loop in main(): (a) Display welcome message. (b) Show the board. (c) Prompt the current player (X goes first). (d) Read input from stdin, parse as number 1-9. (e) Validate input - handle non-numeric input, out-of-range, and occupied cell with clear error messages. (f) Make the move and check for win/draw. (g) Switch players and repeat. (h) When game ends, display the result and ask if players want to play again (y/n).

Step 5: Write comprehensive unit tests in a #[cfg(test)] module within src/main.rs. Tests should cover: (a) New board is empty. (b) Making a valid move updates the board. (c) Making a move on an occupied cell returns error. (d) Horizontal win detection (all 3 rows). (e) Vertical win detection (all 3 columns). (f) Diagonal win detection (both diagonals). (g) Draw detection when board is full with no winner. (h) No winner detected on incomplete board. At minimum 10 tests.

Step 6: Run 'cargo fmt' to format the code, then run 'cargo clippy -- -D warnings' to fix any lint issues, then run 'cargo test' to confirm all tests pass, then run 'cargo build' to confirm compilation.
acceptance_criteria: 'cargo build' compiles successfully with no errors
'cargo test' passes all unit tests (at least 10 tests)
The game uses only the Rust standard library (no external dependencies in Cargo.toml)
Board displays clearly in the terminal with position numbers
Win detection covers rows, columns, and diagonals
Draw detection works when board is full
Invalid input is handled gracefully with error messages
Players can play again after a game ends
